data.body:
## Description

**What**: Merge the open-children check and unblock detection in ticket-transition.sh into a single Python batch operation that shares one reduce_all_tickets call, replacing the bash ticket_find_open_children function that spawns 2n Python processes.
**Why**: ticket transition close currently performs two independent full-tracker scans (open-children via ticket-lib.sh + unblock via ticket-unblock.py), each reducing every ticket. Consolidating into one shared batch reduction cuts the close path from 2 full scans to 1.
**Scope**:
- IN: Replacing ticket_find_open_children bash loop with Python batch; merging open-children + unblock into a single reduce_all_tickets call; ticket-unblock.py scan consolidation
- OUT: Changes to ticket-graph.py (Story 3); changes to the reducer itself

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ticket transition ... closed performs at most one reduce_all_tickets(exclude_archived=True) call (down from two independent full scans)
  ← Satisfies: "ticket transition ... closed performs at most one batch reduction of non-archived tickets"
- When this story is complete, ticket_find_open_children is replaced by a single Python batch operation (no more 2n process spawns)
  ← Satisfies: "ticket_find_open_children no longer spawns 2n Python processes"
- When this story is complete, open-children check and unblock detection share the same batch-reduced ticket state
  ← Satisfies: "ticket transition ... closed performs at most one batch reduction of non-archived tickets"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Performance] ticket-unblock.py (called at line 274 of ticket-transition.sh on close) performs its own full os.scandir + reduce_ticket scan of all tickets (ticket-unblock.py lines 109-119). This scan is independent of the consolidated batch scan. Consider passing the pre-loaded ticket state dict to ticket-unblock.py or inlining its logic to avoid a second full scan on every close operation.
- [Performance] Verify the replacement batch path handles the flock-free read pattern correctly
- [Maintainability] Consolidated batch scan must remain readable — avoid monolithic function

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
